label: Seedance 2 (Video Generation)
description: ByteDance Seedance 2. Text-to-video and image-to-video. Structured prompt format: subject, action, camera, environment, style. Responds best to segmented prompts with kinetic motion verbs.

subject.label: Subject
subject.description: Who or what is in the shot. Include physical appearance and relevant traits. E.g. 'A weathered man in his 60s wearing a yellow slicker'.

action.label: Action / Motion
action.description: What is happening. Use strong motion verbs. E.g. 'leans forward and scrawls coordinates across a damp logbook page'.

camera.label: Camera Instruction
camera.description: Single camera direction. E.g. 'slow push in', 'low tracking shot', 'static wide'. Do not use the word camera in this field.

environment.label: Environment / Lighting
environment.description: Location and lighting mood in one sentence. E.g. 'Stone lighthouse interior, single candle source, deep shadows on salt-worn walls'.

style.label: Style / Technical Notes
style.description: Cinematic style and any technical flags. E.g. 'Cinematic noir, shallow depth of field, 4K'.

negative.label: Negative Prompt
negative.description: What to avoid. E.g. 'blurry, watermark, text overlay, low quality'.

v_setup_injection.note: v_setup tokens are used as fallback if camera/environment fields are empty. If the camera field is populated, v_setup.move is omitted to avoid duplication.

llm_instructions.writing_guide: Write video prompts for Seedance 2 using a segmented structure across five fields. Each field is ONE concept. Keep total assembled prompt under 150 words. Seedance 2 responds strongly to kinetic motion verbs — use 'surges', 'drifts', 'slams', 'crawls', 'tilts', not 'moves' or 'goes'. The camera field should be a single short instruction like 'slow push in' or 'low tracking shot' — Seedance 2 reads this as a discrete parameter. Do not stack more than two adjectives per noun. The environment field is lighting + location in one sentence maximum.

field_guides.subject: Physical description first, then clothing/props if relevant. Lead with the most visually distinctive trait. Skip if the shot has no clear subject (establishing shots, abstract).
field_guides.action: The motion event of the shot. Start with the strongest kinetic verb. This is the most important field — Seedance 2 is motion-first.
field_guides.camera: Single instruction only. Choose from: slow push in, pull back, low tracking shot, static wide, static close-up, overhead crane down, Dutch tilt hold, handheld follow. Do not say 'camera' or 'we see'.
field_guides.environment: Location name + dominant light source + one atmospheric detail. Keep to one sentence.
field_guides.style: Cinematic reference + technical quality notes. E.g. 'Cinematic noir, shallow DOF, 4K'. Two to four terms max.

examples.action: Harlan writes in the logbook by candlelight

output.subject: A weathered fisherman in his 60s with calloused, ink-stained hands
output.action: leans forward and scrawls coordinates across a damp logbook page, wrist dragging through wet ink
output.environment: Stone lighthouse interior, single candle, deep shadows climbing salt-worn walls
output.style: Cinematic noir, shallow depth of field, 4K

examples.action: Fishing boats leave the dock in early morning light

output.action: Three wooden fishing boats drift out from the dock, leaving slow white wakes across still grey water
output.environment: Fishing harbour at dawn, flat pink light on the horizon, fog over the water

do: Use kinetic motion verbs (surges, drifts, slams, crawls, tilts)
Give the camera field a single short instruction
Lead the subject field with the strongest visual trait
Leave subject empty for shots without a clear foreground subject
dont: Stack more than 2 adjectives per noun
Use passive voice for motion ('is seen walking' → 'walks')
Begin the camera field with the word 'camera'
Describe the same detail in multiple fields
